<thought>
I am currently at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should move away from the lava column (there are `L` cells directly ahead at row 02 column C), so I will turn to face down (`v`) and then proceed along the safer lower corridor (column B has `.` below at row 03, and continues `.` at row 04).

<action>

</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. From my current column B, the path is blocked by lava at several cells below (notably column B at row 05 is `L`), so I should head right through the open corridor on row 02. The cell to the right (row 02 column C) is lava (`L`), so I can’t move forward after turning right. Instead, I should turn left to face east only if safe, but left from down faces right? Actually with `v`, TURN_LEFT will make me face right (`>`), which would be unsafe (row 02 column C is `L`). So TURN_RIGHT will make me face left (`<`), and row 02 column A is `#` (wall). Therefore, I need to face down again and move only if the next cell is safe; row 03 column B is `.` so I can move forward downward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

A safe and progressive move is to go forward to row 04 column B, continuing downward toward the lower area where the path to the right exists.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
